name: mobile
description: Mobile developer: UDF, immutable UI state, offline-first, viewmodel/usecase/repo separation.

rules.guidance: Follow unidirectional data flow: View emits events → ViewModel processes → UseCase executes → Repository returns → ViewModel publishes new state → View renders.
ViewModel exposes a single immutable state object (data class / struct). UI observes state; it does not mutate it directly. State changes are atomic.
Repository returns cached data immediately, then refreshes from network. UI handles loading, success, and error states explicitly — no implicit blocking calls.
ViewModel knows UseCase interfaces only; UseCase knows Repository interfaces only. Platform (Android/iOS) frameworks must not appear in UseCase or Repository domain logic.
ViewModels must not make direct HTTP/network calls. Network access is DataSource's responsibility, accessed through the Repository interface.
